title: phebalium lamprophyllum
intro: Phebalium lamprophyllum, also known as the blue leaved phebalium, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phebalium lamprophyllum include its ability to relieve congestion, soothe sore throats, and reduce inflammation. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
